videos.title: China/African Cooperation is Truly a Win-Win Relationship

chapters.title: Lyndon H. LaRouche, Jr. Dr. Karl Marx Refuted (1983)

videos.description: Jason Ross proves China’s massive investments of infrastructure into the nations of Africa are truly beneficial for both parties.  China's Belt and Road Initiative is changing the potential of the future of African nations by breaking from the past of the colonial powers and delivering what the continent actually needs: science, infrastructure and cooperation.

videos.title: Question and Answer with Helga Zepp-LaRouche and Jason Ross

videos.title: Towards a Four Power Agreement For a 'New Paradigm' of Development: Panel One

chapters.title: Africa has the largest development potential
China and Africa work together towards prosperity

videos.description: Helga Zepp-LaRouche keynotes the conference providing her unique insight into the dynamic fight between the "New Silk Road Spirit" sweeping the globe typified by the recent India and Pakistan developments, or at the summits between the U.S., the Republic of Korea and the DPRK, versus the adherents of the Old Paradigm of geopolitics that continue to foment conflict.  Jason Ross follows next, proving China's massive investment into infrastructure into the nations of Africa is truly beneficial for both parties.  The conference firmly establishes the capability to avert tragedy in this momentous occasion in human history and create a 'New Paradigm' in human relations.

videos.title: Towards a Four Power Agreement For a 'New Paradigm' of Development: Panel Two
videos.description: The second panel includes State Senator Richard Black (R-VA10), James George Jatras, former U.S. diplomat, Dmitry Polyanskiy, First Deputy Permanent Rep of the Russian Federation to the UN, Roger Stone, adviser to the Trump faction of the Republican Party and Helga Zepp-LaRouche.  Senator Black and James Jatras deliver sharp warnings about the risk of the U.S. intervening on the side of al-Qaeda in the conflict surrounding Syria's Idlib province.  Mr. Polyanskiy delivers a brief but poignant message ensuring that talks are continuing between the U.S. and Russia.  Roger Stone presents his unique background in the Republican Party and admiration of the LaRouche's fight for a just economic system.  A couple lively Q&A sessions involving Mrs. LaRouche, Jason Ross of the first panel, Roger Stone and audience participants ensues throughout the panel.
